The Global Blueberry Atlas

What the world grows, ships, and pays through the year — and exactly what you can know about it for free. Reference year 2023.

The year

The relay — who supplies the world each month

Chile owns Jan–Feb, Spain the Northern spring, the US & Canada the summer, and Peru the great counter-season surge of Sep–Nov.

UN Comtrade monthly · supply-side export volume · free

The index

Every country on one screen — click any column to sort, click a name to jump to its card. Blank cells (—) are the gaps: no FAOSTAT production, no forecast; ᵖ = provisional latest year (Comtrade still finalising). The coverage bar shows each country's source mix (✅ wired · 🟢 free · 💷 paid · ⛔ none).

Countries & coverage (32 profiles)

Each country leads with the facts everyone wants — volume (exports & imports on one axis, the smaller flow shaded) and price — then partners, depth, and a coverage card. Price is the customs unit-value (value ÷ net weight: FOB for exports, CIF for imports) — an average border price, not retail or spot. Named exporters where the depth exists.

The atlas — what you can know

The registry as a map: every country × data-class, flagged by access. This — not the data — is what no one else publishes.

held — free & wired29
axis covered by a base layer*46
true headroom (unwired, mostly fragile)27
info-only — not a dataset16
paid only20
no source (structural gap)24

*national customs → Comtrade/COMEXT · national area → FAOSTAT · weather → NASA POWER: the data-point’s axis is already measured globally; the national source would add only granularity, not new signal.

Built 2026-06-19 from free, public sources (UN Comtrade · FAOSTAT · USDA-FAS · NASA POWER · Eurostat · national NPPOs). Every figure is stamped with its source and access. Latest ~2 years of trade are provisional. · The Global Blueberry Atlas · fruit is a parameter.
